analyzeResult.content:
Docusign Envelope ID: 6138F5BD-8039-4143-AB75-DF621C01A1FF
Contract No. 25000012048
OF RICHMOND *
IT
ESTABLIS
1737
JED
CITY OF RICHMOND GOODS AND SERVICES CONTRACT
This Contract, dated this 28th day of July , 2025, (the "Commencement Date") between the City of Richmond, Virginia (the "City"), Insight Public Sector (the "Contractor"), and Flock Group Inc. ("Flock") is binding among and between these parties as of the date of the City's final signature.
WHEREAS, Cobb County Contract No. 23-6692-03 was awarded by Cobb County, Georgia as the Principal Procurement Agency, on behalf of OMNIA Partners, Public Sector to Contractor effective May 1, 2023, pursuant to Solicitation No. 23-6692 (the "Cobb County Contract No. 23-6692-03");
WHEREAS, in connection with the City's purchase, Flock requires execution of its End-User Software License Agreement ("EULA") solely to facilitate the provision of certain technology products and services resold through the Contractor;
WHEREAS, the City desires to exercise its authority pursuant to section 21-40(b)(1) of the Code of the City of Richmond to make purchases off of the Cobb County Contract;
THEREFORE, in consideration of the Recitals set forth above and good and valuable consideration as set forth below, the parties agree as follows:
1. Scope of Contract. The Contractor shall provide the goods and services to the City as set forth in the Contract Documents enumerated in Section 3 below.
2. Contract Amount. The maximum authorized contract amount for this Contract is Two Hundred Seventy-Six Thousand and 00/100 U. S. dollars ($276,000.00). The aggregate of all payments by the City under this Contract shall not exceed this amount. All payments shall be as provided in the Contract Documents. All payments and other obligations by the City under this Contract are subject to annual appropriations by the Richmond City Council; consequently, this Contract shall bind the City only to the extent that Richmond City Council appropriates sufficient funds for the City to perform its obligations hereunder.
3. Contract Documents. This Contract shall consist of the following Contract Documents, listed in order of precedence from highest to lowest:
‘
A. This Goods and Services Contract between the City and the Contractor.
B. The Cobb County Contract 23-6692-03.
C. Contractor Quote No. 0228418552 dated May 2
D. , 2025, consisting of two pages, and attached hereto as Attachment A.
E. Flock Safety End-User Software License Agreement, consisting of seven pages, and attached hereto as Attachment B.
F. Any purchase orders issued by the City pursuant to this Contract.
Page 1 of 8
Docusign Envelope ID: 6138F5BD-8039-4143-AB75-DF621C01A1FF
Contract No. 25000012048
All of these documents are incorporated herein by reference.
3. Additional Provisions. The parties further agree as follows:
A. Definitions. Whenever the term "Lead Public Agency," "Cobb County," "Owner," "the County," "Principal Procurement Agency," "Participating Public Agency," or "Public Agencies" appears in the Contract Documents, it shall have the same meaning as "City" for purposes of this contractual relationship between the City and the Contractor.
B. Payments.
1. The Contractor shall submit invoices that include (i) a unique invoice number, (ii) the City's purchase order number, and (iii) the Contractor's Federal Tax Identification Number to either accountspayable@rva.gov or City of Richmond, Accounts Payable, 900 East Broad Street, Richmond, VA 23219.
2. The City shall pay the Contractor as follows: Net 45 days.
3. Method of Ordering.
a. The City may place orders pursuant to this Contract by requesting the Contractor provide a quote, which shall be incorporated into the Contract upon the issuance of a Purchase Order to which the quote is attached. Each quote provided by the Contract must (i) identify this Contract by reference to the City Contract Number; (ii) identify the Cobb County Contract No. 23-6692-03 by reference; (iii) identify the license, equipment, products, or services being ordered or renewed by the City; (iv) identify any applicable term or renewal period (start and end dates), (v) identify the price per license or product, or the total renewal cost and set forth any payments to be made to the Contractor by the City, and (vi) state that the terms and conditions of this Contract are controlling.
For equipment requiring installation, the term shall not commence until the City has confirmed successful installation and accepted the equipment in writing. Acceptance shall not be unreasonably withheld and will occur upon the City's verification that the equipment is installed, operational, and in material conformance with the specifications set forth in the quote or applicable documentation. Upon acceptance, the City and Contractor shall document the acceptance date, and the term associated with the equipment shall be deemed to begin on that date and shall be incorporated into the Contract. Any subsequent quote submitted for renewal of such equipment must reflect the term start date established upon acceptance.
Pricing for licenses, equipment, products, and services ordered under this section shall be in accordance with the prices established under Cobb County Contract No. 23-6692-03. Notwithstanding any provisions appearing on or incorporated into the Contractor's quote, the parties agree that such provisions shall not have any effect or be enforceable against the City to the extent the terms are in addition to, or in conflict of, the terms of this Contract which shall govern all transactions between the parties.
Page 2 of 8
